conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with the first one: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I have one child - my son Alex. He's 13 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To continue assessing your eligibility, I need to ask:

**Is Alex living with you, and are you responsible for him?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex does live with me, but his other parent also lives with us and they already claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. This is an important detail because generally, only one person can receive Child Benefit for the same child at any given time.

To clarify your situation: **Are you asking whether you could claim Child Benefit instead of the other parent who currently claims it, or are you asking if you can both claim it for Alex at the same time?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm just trying to find out if I'm eligible to claim it. I didn't realize only one person could claim - so I suppose I'm asking if I could claim it instead of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Let me ask you one more question to fully assess your eligibility:

**Are you a UK resident, or do you otherwise meet the residence requirements (such as working in the UK or receiving certain UK benefits)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

While Alex is the right age (13 years old) and lives with you, there are two key issues:

1. **UK Residence Requirement**: To claim Child Benefit, you must either:
   - Live in the UK, OR
   - Work in the UK and pay UK National Insurance, OR
   - Receive certain UK benefits

   Since you're living abroad and haven't indicated you work in the UK or receive UK benefits, you don't meet this fundamental eligibility requirement.

2. **Single Claimant Rule**: Only one person can receive Child Benefit for the same child at any time. Alex's other parent is already claiming it for him.

Even if you did meet the residence requirement, you would need to discuss with the other parent who should claim, as you couldn't both claim simultaneously. However, the residence issue is the primary barrier to your eligibility in this case.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: To be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex, you must meet several criteria: 1) You must be responsible for a child under 16 (or under 20 if they stay in approved education or training) - Alex is 13, so this is satisfied. 2) You must live in the UK, or work in the UK and pay UK National Insurance, or receive certain UK benefits - you have stated you are living abroad and have not indicated you work in the UK or receive UK benefits, so this residence requirement is not met. 3) Only one person can get Child Benefit for a child, and Alex's other parent is already claiming it. Even if you met the residence requirement, you would need to be the primary caregiver and claim priority over the current claimant. Since you do not meet the UK residence requirement, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 13), who lives with them along with the other parent who currently claims Child Benefit. The claimant does not meet the UK residence requirement as they are living abroad and have not indicated they work in the UK or receive UK benefits. This is a fundamental eligibility requirement for Child Benefit. Therefore, they are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
